hypotheses: Move the accepted v1.6 search structure onto a lower-cost native board representation so later C# search and evaluation experiments are meaningful under fixed time budgets.
versions.implementation_summary: Native-board C# v2 baseline with iterative deepening, negamax alpha-beta, quiescence search, and transposition-table reuse.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v2.0 scored 207.0/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 160 wins, 94 draws, 246 losses, score rate 0.4140.

versions.summary: Native-board C# v2 baseline with iterative deepening, negamax alpha-beta, quiescence search, and transposition-table reuse.

hypotheses: A lightweight quiet-history move-ordering table will improve alpha-beta cutoffs at 100ms per move without changing evaluation.
versions.summary: Cloned v2.0 into v2.1, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, and added a per-search quiet history table that rewards quiet beta-cutoff moves by side/from/to square.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v2.0 into v2.1, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, and added a per-search quiet history table that rewards quiet beta-cutoff moves by side/from/to square.

stockfish_1350.text: No final Stockfish (1350 Elo) result was recorded for this rejected attempt.

hypotheses: A compact piece-square positional evaluation term will improve direct move choice at 100ms per move more reliably than search-only ordering tweaks.
versions.summary: Cloned v2.0 into v2.2, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, and added static piece-square tables for pawns, knights, bishops, rooks, queens, and phase-blended kings to the local evaluation.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v2.0 into v2.2, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, and added static piece-square tables for pawns, knights, bishops, rooks, queens, and phase-blended kings to the local evaluation.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v2.2 scored 292.0/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 117 wins, 350 draws, 33 losses, score rate 0.5840.

hypotheses: Caching the endgame phase for king piece-square scoring will preserve v2.2 evaluation semantics while increasing searched nodes enough to improve paired results.
versions.summary: Cloned v2.2 into v2.3, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, and changed evaluation snapshot construction to compute endgame phase once, collect king squares during the existing board scan, and score king PSTs from the cached phase instead of rescanning the board.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v2.2 into v2.3, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, and changed evaluation snapshot construction to compute endgame phase once, collect king squares during the existing board scan, and score king PSTs from the cached phase instead of rescanning the board.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v2.3 scored 244.5/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 65 wins, 359 draws, 76 losses, score rate 0.4890.

hypotheses: A small bishop-pair bonus will improve positional decisions on top of v2.2 with negligible extra evaluation cost.
versions.summary: Cloned v2.2 into v2.4, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, and added a 35 centipawn bishop-pair bonus to each side's existing positional total after bishop counts are collected.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v2.2 into v2.4, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, and added a 35 centipawn bishop-pair bonus to each side's existing positional total after bishop counts are collected.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v2.4 scored 254.0/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 80 wins, 348 draws, 72 losses, score rate 0.5080.

hypotheses: A modest passed-pawn advancement bonus will improve conversion and pawn-structure decisions beyond v2.2's indirect endgame pawn-danger term.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v2.2 into v2.5 and added rank-scaled passed-pawn bonuses for pawns with no opposing pawn ahead on the same or adjacent files.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v2.5 scored 300.0/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 239 wins, 122 draws, 139 losses, score rate 0.6000.

versions.summary: Cloned v2.2 into v2.5 and added rank-scaled passed-pawn bonuses for pawns with no opposing pawn ahead on the same or adjacent files.

hypotheses: Protected passed pawns should be valued slightly more than bare passed pawns because they are harder to blockade and more likely to convert.
versions.summary: Cloned v2.5 into v2.6, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, and added a 14 centipawn bonus when a passed pawn is defended from behind by a friendly pawn on an adjacent file.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v2.5 into v2.6, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, and added a 14 centipawn bonus when a passed pawn is defended from behind by a friendly pawn on an adjacent file.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v2.6 scored 254.0/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 93 wins, 322 draws, 85 losses, score rate 0.5080.

hypotheses: A small doubled-pawn penalty may improve pawn-structure decisions without stacking more passed-pawn bonuses on top of v2.5.
versions.summary: Cloned v2.5 into v2.7, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, counted pawns per file during evaluation, and subtracted a 12 centipawn penalty for each extra same-color pawn on a file.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v2.5 into v2.7, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, counted pawns per file during evaluation, and subtracted a 12 centipawn penalty for each extra same-color pawn on a file.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v2.7 scored 248.5/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 93 wins, 311 draws, 96 losses, score rate 0.4970.

hypotheses: A modest rook open-file and semi-open-file bonus will improve rook activity and conversion decisions beyond v2.5 without materially slowing evaluation.
versions.summary: Cloned v2.5 into v2.8, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, counted pawn files during evaluation, and added 18 centipawns for rooks on open files or 9 centipawns for rooks on semi-open files.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v2.5 into v2.8, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, counted pawn files during evaluation, and added 18 centipawns for rooks on open files or 9 centipawns for rooks on semi-open files.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v2.8 scored 302.0/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 247 wins, 110 draws, 143 losses, score rate 0.6040.

hypotheses: A modest knight outpost bonus will improve minor-piece activity decisions on top of v2.8 without materially slowing evaluation.
Using pawn-defended and not enemy-pawn-attacked advanced knight squares is more targeted than another broad pawn-structure bonus.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v2.8 into v2.9 and added a knight outpost bonus for advanced, pawn-defended squares not attacked by enemy pawns.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v2.9 scored 324.0/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 271 wins, 106 draws, 123 losses, score rate 0.6480.

versions.summary: Cloned v2.8 into v2.9 and added a knight outpost bonus for advanced, pawn-defended squares not attacked by enemy pawns.

hypotheses: A small rook-on-seventh-rank activity bonus may improve conversion and attacking pressure on top of v2.9's open-file rook logic.
versions.summary: Cloned v2.9 into v2.10, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, and added a 16 centipawn bonus for rooks on the opponent's second rank.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v2.9 into v2.10, renamed the public type/search entrypoint, and added a 16 centipawn bonus for rooks on the opponent's second rank.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v2.10 scored 304.5/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 256 wins, 97 draws, 147 losses, score rate 0.6090.

hypotheses: Persisting the native transposition table across moves within a game should reuse prior search work and improve move ordering/cutoffs compared with v2.9's per-move fresh TT allocation.

Checking a normalized FEN opening lookup before native search should avoid spending the 100ms move budget on early-book positions while preserving legal SAN output through the existing BoardState move path.
versions.implementation_summary: Forked v2.9 into V3_0Engine with optional per-game search context and opening-book lookup before the native search path.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v3.0 scored 305.5/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 254 wins, 103 draws, 143 losses, score rate 0.6110.

limitations: Context reuse depends on the same warm server instance receiving future requests, so cold starts or different instances may begin with an empty cache.
versions.summary: Forked v2.9 into V3_0Engine with optional per-game search context and opening-book lookup before the native search path.

hypotheses: A targeted king-shelter term that rewards a real pawn shield in front of a home-rank king and penalizes open or semi-open nearby files will improve middlegame king safety and move choice on top of v3.0.
versions.summary: Cloned v3.0 into v3.1, renamed the public type and search-context entrypoints, and added a middlegame-scaled king-shelter evaluation bonus that inspects the pawn shield directly in front of a back-rank king and penalizes missing shelter on nearby open or semi-open files.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v3.0 into v3.1, renamed the public type and search-context entrypoints, and added a middlegame-scaled king-shelter evaluation bonus that inspects the pawn shield directly in front of a back-rank king and penalizes missing shelter on nearby open or semi-open files.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v3.1 scored 188.0/325 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 140 wins, 56 draws, 129 losses, score rate 0.5785.

hypotheses: A generation-aware transposition-table replacement policy will preserve fresher entries in the persistent v3 table and improve move quality at 100ms without adding much per-node cost.
versions.summary: Added v3.2 as a focused transposition-table experiment that changes TT storage to prefer same-generation updates instead of blindly replacing persistent entries.
versions.implementation_summary: Added v3.2 as a focused transposition-table experiment that changes TT storage to prefer same-generation updates instead of blindly replacing persistent entries.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v3.2 scored 290.5/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 245 wins, 91 draws, 164 losses, score rate 0.5810.

hypotheses: A cheap bishop safe-mobility term will improve minor-piece activity decisions on top of v3.0 without the high per-node cost seen in the rejected v3.1 king-shelter scan.
Rewarding bounded diagonal reach to non-edge squares that are not controlled by enemy pawns is more targeted than retrying a generic bishop-pair bonus or another broad static piece-placement term.
versions.summary: Cloned v3.0 into v3.3, renamed the public type and search-context entrypoints, and added a capped bishop safe-mobility evaluation bonus that counts diagonal reachable non-edge squares not attacked by enemy pawns.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v3.0 into v3.3, renamed the public type and search-context entrypoints, and added a capped bishop safe-mobility evaluation bonus that counts diagonal reachable non-edge squares not attacked by enemy pawns.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v3.3 scored 302.5/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 251 wins, 103 draws, 146 losses, score rate 0.6050.

hypotheses: Making draw/repetition contempt material-aware will improve score by letting materially worse positions prefer repetition or 50-move draw chances instead of steering away from saves.
Keeping the existing draw-avoidance penalties for equal-or-better positions should preserve v3.0's decisiveness while reducing avoidable losses.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v3.0 into v3.4 and changed RepetitionDrawAdjustment so materially worse positions receive bounded draw-saving bonuses while equal-or-better positions retain repetition and draw penalties.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v3.4 scored 323.0/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 277 wins, 92 draws, 131 losses, score rate 0.6460.

limitations: Context reuse depends on the same warm server instance receiving future requests, so cold starts or different instances may begin with an empty cache.
versions.summary: Cloned v3.0 into v3.4 and changed RepetitionDrawAdjustment so materially worse positions receive bounded draw-saving bonuses while equal-or-better positions retain repetition and draw penalties.

hypotheses: A principal-variation search pass on non-first moves will let v3.5 search more narrowly behind the existing TT move ordering, improving effective depth at 100ms without adding evaluation overhead.
Applying the same narrow-window re-search policy at the root should preserve move quality while reducing wasted full-window work on clearly inferior alternatives.
versions.summary: Cloned v3.4 into v3.5 and changed the root and recursive negamax loops to use principal-variation style zero-window searches on non-first ordered moves, with full re-search only when a narrow probe beats the current alpha/best score.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v3.4 into v3.5 and changed the root and recursive negamax loops to use principal-variation style zero-window searches on non-first ordered moves, with full re-search only when a narrow probe beats the current alpha/best score.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v3.5 scored 356.5/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 320 wins, 73 draws, 107 losses, score rate 0.7130.

hypotheses: Root aspiration windows around the previous iteration score will reduce full-window work on stable positions and improve effective depth at 100ms, while fail-low and fail-high re-search keeps the result exact when the window is wrong.
versions.summary: Cloned v3.5 into v3.6 and wrapped iterative deepening root searches in aspiration windows centered on the previous iteration score, with a factored root-search helper and automatic widening re-search on fail-low or fail-high before storing the exact root result.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v3.5 into v3.6 and wrapped iterative deepening root searches in aspiration windows centered on the previous iteration score, with a factored root-search helper and automatic widening re-search on fail-low or fail-high before storing the exact root result.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v3.6 scored 367.5/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 343 wins, 49 draws, 108 losses, score rate 0.7350.

hypotheses: A persistent quiet-move ordering layer built from killer moves and quiet history will improve v3.6's PVS and aspiration-window cutoffs at 100ms without adding per-node evaluation cost.
versions.summary: Cloned v3.6 into v3.7 and extended the search context with persistent killer-move and quiet-history tables, then fed those heuristics into quiet move ordering and updated them on quiet beta cutoffs at the root and recursive negamax nodes.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v3.6 into v3.7 and extended the search context with persistent killer-move and quiet-history tables, then fed those heuristics into quiet move ordering and updated them on quiet beta cutoffs at the root and recursive negamax nodes.

stockfish_1350.text: No final Stockfish (1350 Elo) result was recorded for this rejected attempt.

hypotheses: Persistent killer-move ordering for quiet beta cutoffs will improve v3.6's PVS and aspiration-window searches at 100ms by getting strong refutations to the front faster in later sibling nodes.

A side-aware quiet-history table keyed by from/to squares will reinforce repeatedly successful quiet cutoffs across positions without changing evaluation cost, giving better move ordering than transposition-table moves alone.
versions.summary: Cloned v3.6 into v3.8 and added persistent primary/secondary killer moves plus a side-aware quiet-history table to the search context, then used those heuristics to score quiet move ordering and reward quiet beta cutoffs at the root and recursive negamax nodes while keeping the change self-contained in the engine file.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v3.6 into v3.8 and added persistent primary/secondary killer moves plus a side-aware quiet-history table to the search context, then used those heuristics to score quiet move ordering and reward quiet beta cutoffs at the root and recursive negamax nodes while keeping the change self-contained in the engine file.

stockfish_1350.text: No final Stockfish (1350 Elo) result was recorded for this rejected attempt.

hypotheses: Internal iterative deepening at deeper non-check nodes with no transposition-table move will seed a useful hash move for v3.6's existing PVS ordering, improving cutoffs at 100ms without adding evaluation cost.
versions.summary: Cloned v3.6 into v3.9 and added a contained internal-iterative-deepening fallback in negamax so deeper non-check nodes without a TT move first run a reduced-depth probe to populate and reuse a transposition-table move for the main ordered search.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v3.6 into v3.9 and added a contained internal-iterative-deepening fallback in negamax so deeper non-check nodes without a TT move first run a reduced-depth probe to populate and reuse a transposition-table move for the main ordered search.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v3.9 scored 367.0/500 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 329 wins, 76 draws, 95 losses, score rate 0.7340.

hypotheses: Late-move reductions for quiet, later-ordered moves should let v3.10 search deeper on low-priority branches without weakening principal-variation re-search when a reduced move proves interesting.
A narrow killer-move ordering layer for quiet beta cutoffs should improve v3.10's existing PVS move ordering enough to make those late-move reductions safer and more effective at 100ms.
versions.summary: Cloned v3.6 into v3.10 and added quiet-move killer ordering plus a contained late-move reduction path for later-ordered quiet moves, with full PVS re-search whenever a reduced probe still raises alpha.
versions.implementation_summary: Cloned v3.6 into v3.10 and added quiet-move killer ordering plus a contained late-move reduction path for later-ordered quiet moves, with full PVS re-search whenever a reduced probe still raises alpha.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v3.10 scored 747.0/1000 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 682 wins, 130 draws, 188 losses, score rate 0.7470.

hypotheses: Adding a lightweight bishop mobility term will improve piece-activity move selection more reliably than another generic one-off static bonus.
Rewarding king-supported advanced passed pawns and penalizing directly blockaded passed pawns in endgames will improve conversion and reduce wasted winning lines.
versions.summary: Added bishop mobility scoring, added an endgame passed-pawn escort/blockade evaluation term based on king proximity to the square in front of advanced passers, and refactored king PST scoring to use the already-computed endgame phase inside the evaluation snapshot.
versions.implementation_summary: Added bishop mobility scoring, added an endgame passed-pawn escort/blockade evaluation term based on king proximity to the square in front of advanced passers, and refactored king PST scoring to use the already-computed endgame phase inside the evaluation snapshot.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v3.11 scored 756.5/1000 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 685 wins, 143 draws, 172 losses, score rate 0.7565.

hypotheses: A modest rook-on-seventh-rank activity bonus should improve practical attacking and conversion move choice with minimal evaluation overhead.
Rewarding connected advanced passed pawns in endgames should improve promotion-race and winning-line conversion beyond the existing single-passer escort/blockade term.
versions.summary: Added a rook-on-seventh-rank activity bonus with an extra incentive when the opposing king is stuck on the back rank, and added an endgame-weighted connected passed-pawn bonus for adjacent advanced passers while reusing the evaluator's existing passed-pawn tracking.
versions.implementation_summary: Added a rook-on-seventh-rank activity bonus with an extra incentive when the opposing king is stuck on the back rank, and added an endgame-weighted connected passed-pawn bonus for adjacent advanced passers while reusing the evaluator's existing passed-pawn tracking.

stockfish_1350.text: C# v3.12 scored 752.5/1000 against Stockfish (1350 Elo): 677 wins, 151 draws, 172 losses, score rate 0.7525.
